Concatenate Multiple Inputs in an FFmpeg Pipeline

Source: R/ffm.R
ffm_concat.Rd

Join the pipeline's input files one after another using FFmpeg's concat demuxer. This is a blessed multi-input verb (like ffm_hstack): it stream-copies, so it is fast and lossless but requires that every input share the same parameters (codec, resolution, frame rate, ...). To concatenate inputs with differing parameters you must re-encode via the concat filter (not yet wrapped; use the Layer 0 escape hatch).

Usage

ffm_concat(object)

Arguments

object

An ffmpeg pipeline (ffm) object created by ffm_files() with more than one input file.

Value

object with the added instruction to concatenate the inputs.

Details

The demuxer needs a list file naming the inputs; ffm_concat() writes one to a temporary path immediately and stores it in the pipeline, so the compiled command can reference it. It also copies codecs and maps all streams (as ffm_copy would).

See also

Other builder functions: ffm(), ffm_batch(), ffm_codec(), ffm_compile(), ffm_copy(), ffm_crop(), ffm_drawbox(), ffm_drop(), ffm_files(), ffm_fps(), ffm_hstack(), ffm_loudnorm(), ffm_map(), ffm_output_options(), ffm_overlay(), ffm_pixel_format(), ffm_run(), ffm_scale(), ffm_seek(), ffm_trim(), ffm_vstack(), print.tidymedia_ffm()

Examples

video <- system.file("extdata", "sample.mp4", package = "tidymedia")
# Join two inputs end-to-end (they must share codec/resolution/frame rate)
ffm(c(video, video), "output.mp4") |>
  ffm_concat() |>
  ffm_compile()
#> [1] "-y -f concat -safe 0 -i \"/tmp/Rtmp0nD5H9/ffm-concat1fae2f7073b2.txt\" -codec:v copy -codec:a copy -map 0 \"output.mp4\""
